name: Community Bank Of Missouri
description_short: Community Bank of Missouri is a locally-owned bank chartered in 2001, offering deposit accounts, mortgages, and commercial lending with direct access to management.
description_long:
Community Bank of Missouri was founded in 2001 by D.R. and Stephanie Landwehr alongside local investors Gregg Steele and Gary Arnette, chartered in Richmond, Missouri. The bank was created to address a recognized need for a locally-focused financial institution embedded in its community. After 25 years of operation, it remains locally owned and operated with the same community-minded approach that guided its founding. The bank celebrates this milestone with events scheduled throughout 2026.

The bank offers a range of financial products and services including deposit accounts, FREE Student Checking, online and mobile banking through CBOM Mobile (available on iOS and Android), mortgages, construction loans, and commercial financing. They provide personal financial calculators and rate information online, with the ability to apply for products directly through their website. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

Community Bank of Missouri distinguishes itself through direct access to an eight-person management team with over 200 years of combined banking experience. The founders and leadership bring deep expertise—D.R. Landwehr began his banking career in 1970 with experience as a bank president and workout specialist who has navigated multiple economic crises. Stephanie Landwehr, who began her career in 1978, holds the position of President/COO and has experience across all banking operations. The bank emphasizes local decision-making authority and the ability to act decisively on complex financial transactions. They position themselves as "big enough to provide all the services and features you might expect of a larger bank, but small enough to know your name."

Based on publicly available information, Community Bank of Missouri operates as a traditional community bank with standard offerings. The website provides limited detail on specific loan terms, rates, or comprehensive product specifications. As a small, locally-focused institution, their service area and availability may be geographically limited to Missouri communities. Prospective customers should contact the bank directly regarding current rates, specific loan products, and availability in their area. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Eight-person management team with over 200 years of combined banking experience
Locally-owned and operated with local decision-making authority for faster loan processing
Direct access to bank leadership for complex financial transactions
Mobile banking through CBOM Mobile app (iOS and Android)
Offers construction loans and commercial financing alongside residential mortgages
cons: Limited geographic footprint as a small community bank—likely only serves Missouri locations
Website provides minimal detail on current rates, loan terms, or specific product features
No information about fees, minimum balances, or account requirements disclosed online
Small institution may have fewer ATM locations and less extensive branch network than larger banks
best_for: Missouri residents and business owners seeking personalized service and local decision-making
Commercial businesses and construction projects requiring relationship-based lending
Customers prioritizing direct access to bank leadership for complex financial needs
diagnosis: Community Bank of Missouri is best for Missouri-based individuals and businesses seeking a relationship-focused banking experience with direct access to experienced leadership. The main caveat is that as a small, locally-owned institution, they likely have limited geographic availability and the website lacks transparency on specific rates, fees, and product terms that require direct contact with the bank.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
